assertion_results.text: The plan explicitly references reusing common/src/db/query.rs::apply_filter for comma-separated multi-value query parameter parsing rather than writing new parsing logic (constraint 5.4)

This reuses the existing shared helper rather than writing custom parsing or SQL.' Also plan line 153: 'common/src/db/query.rs::apply_filter -- call directly for comma-separated parsing and SQL IN clause generation.' Reuse analysis section 1 (lines 11-33) provides detailed explanation of how apply_filter is reused for comma-separated multi-value parsing.

assertion_results.text: The plan references using entity/src/package_license.rs for the JOIN query rather than writing raw SQL or creating a new entity
assertion_results.evidence: Plan lines 143-146: 'Add a JOIN to the package_license table (using the entity::package_license SeaORM entity from entity/src/package_license.rs) to filter packages by their associated license SPDX identifiers.' Plan lines 155-156: 'entity/src/package_license.rs -- use the SeaORM entity for the JOIN rather than writing raw SQL.' Reuse analysis section 3 (lines 73-90) explains: 'Instead of writing raw SQL like JOIN package_license ON package.id = package_license.package_id, the implementation uses SeaORM’s type-safe join API with the package_license entity.'

assertion_results.text: The reuse analysis covers all 3 Reuse Candidates from the task description, explaining how each would be applied
assertion_results.evidence: reuse-analysis.md contains three numbered sections: '1. common/src/db/query.rs::apply_filter' (lines 11-33), '2. modules/fundamental/src/advisory/endpoints/list.rs (Severity Filter Pattern)' (lines 37-69), '3. entity/src/package_license.rs (Package-License Entity)' (lines 73-96). Each section includes 'What it provides', 'How it is reused', and 'Why reuse is correct' subsections with detailed explanations of application.

Reuse analysis lines 30-33: 'Alternative considered and rejected: Writing a custom parse_license_filter function that splits on commas and builds the query. Rejected because apply_filter already does exactly this, and introducing a parallel implementation would create duplication.'
